title.zh: 尼日利亞民兵的生死密碼：社區凝聚力如何決定自衛成效？
title.en: The Double-Edged Sword of Civilian Militias: Community Capacity Determines Survival in Nigeria's Boko Haram Conflict

summary.zh:
尼日利亞東北部的博科哈拉姆衝突中，民間自衛隊伍的命運截然不同：有的成功守護社區，有的卻淪為暴力的幫兇。為何同樣是民兵組織，結果卻天差地別？這篇發表於《Political Geography》的研究揭示了關鍵——社區的「集體行動能力」，即地方領袖的號召力與社會凝聚力。高能力社區能自主組建民兵，並透過內部約束確保行動有效，成為百姓的「防護盾」；低能力社區則被迫接受政府強推的民兵，卻無法管控成員，結果民兵反而成了暴行的「火藥桶」。

這項研究挑戰了傳統觀點：保護平民不僅依賴外部軍事支持，更需要本地社群的自主凝聚力。當國家壓制性太強，會激發民間武裝抵抗，但社區能否成功組織，取決於其自身的社會基礎。就像洪水來臨時，若堤壩（社區凝聚力）堅固，民眾能自發築堤抗洪；一旦堤壩潰決，再強的軍隊也難以阻止災害蔓延。這揭示了在戰亂中，民間自衛的真正密碼始終掌握在社區自身手中。
summary.en:
In Nigeria's protracted conflict with Boko Haram, civilian militias have emerged as a paradoxical force: while some groups successfully shield communities from violence, others intensify rebel attacks. A new study in *Political Geography* reveals that the critical factor lies in a community's collective action capacity—the strength of local leadership and social cohesion. In areas where communities possess these capacities, they form community-initiated militias that maintain internal oversight, enabling effective protection against rebels. By contrast, in communities with weak social cohesion, state-imposed militias lack control, rendering them ineffective and often exacerbating violence.

The research challenges the assumption that external military support alone can protect civilians, highlighting instead the indispensable role of grassroots agency. When state repression creates incentives for civilian resistance, the ability of communities to organize and sustain self-defense becomes the determining factor. This insight not only explains why some militias succeed while others fail but also underscores that even in violent conflicts, the capacities enabling nonviolent resistance can empower communities to form effective armed groups—a double-edged sword that, when wielded by cohesive communities, becomes a lifeline rather than a liability.

keyArticle.title: Civilian militia formation and protection against rebel violence: Evidence from Nigeria
